They mapped the tainted image into a section, rolled back the TxF changes to restore the on-disk file, then spawned a process from the in-memory section to run their payload under the guise of the legitimate binary.

This aligns closely with the technique of "Masquerading" (T1036), which involves making a malicious file or command resemble a legitimate one to better blend in with normal traffic or processes.
